reviews.review_msg: Terrible experience. She is vague with her expectations pertaining to exams and assignments. She has spelling errors on questions which are very language sensitive. To top it off, she's passive aggressive and downright rude sometimes in her feedback to students. I've never seen a worse attitude towards students by a university professor. Appalling.

DO NOT take a class with this professor. I have never been so frustrated trying to understand course content. She is very difficult to get a hold of (e-mailed her 4 times before she responded) and responded to my questions with "maybe you will understand once the course is done" when I finally got an appointment with her.

Do not take a class with this professor. I have never felt so frustrated trying to understand course material. She is hard to get a hold of (emailed her 4 times before I got a response) and told me "maybe you will understand after the course is over" instead of answering my questions. Take the course with someone else!

She's very hard to get a hold of. Twice she just didn't respond to my emails at all. She's easy to understand in class but doesn't post info about projects, like what she expects & how she grades. Her test didn't ask one straightforward question. I studied & was still confused at what she was getting at. She seems nice until you ask her for help.

One of the best and nicest profs I've had. She is extremely helpful and concerned about her students. No trick questions and surprises in the exams. She makes the most difficult courses seem easy and gives you a great grade. I highly recommend this course for an easy grade.

She is one of the best professors I've had so far. very concerned about her students and helpful. she's not the kind of prof that tricks you with stupid questions in the exams. easy to get a good grade.

Absolutely brutal class, do not take if you do not need it! Professor is not helpful at all, labs and lectures do not coincide, the textbook taught me more than lectures did. Not clear in lectures/ explaining concepts. So. BAD!

Professor Dasovic is a super funny prof and understands that us IS kids are NOT meant to take stats and research. Although lectures are pretty boring he gives you pretty clear cut definitions of all the terms you will need to know for the midterm and final. Expect lots of research projects throughout the semester.

Exams are your grade booster for this class. They are solely based on his lectures so make sure you attend them. His assignments are hell in order to survive them make sure you form a study group. Once you have a study group setup you should be okay. This class can be a GPA booster if you just put time and effort into it. Take only taking 3 classes

Unorganized & assignments were too long. Does provide help but replies r vague & by end of semester was unhelpfulignored students. Definitely make a study group becuz will take group effort to finish assignments. However exams were not too difficult just study lecture notes altho textbook provides examples. Try to get into TA tutorial & not his.
He doesn't just give answers away, he teaches you to think.

Engaging, great lecturer, and if you go to his office hours, very helpful. If you go out of your way to contact him, he's willing to go out of his way to help you. While he could be better organised, I feel like that's just because this was his first semester back after taking a break from teaching for a while. Loved taking IS 240 & IS 210 with him

For an extremely boring subject I had no interest in, Dasovic makes it better than tolerable. Some people react negatively to his style, he can bounce around a little, I personally like it. He tries hard to keep things more engaging, always answers questions, and is usually quick with email responses. The course itself = awful, Dasovic = great.

The fact negative comments are referring to him as a "teacher" just shows the intelligence level of people taking his classes. Dasovic is an amazing PROFESSOR. He wants you to do well and is incredibly helpful. Exams are very very fair and had the potential to be harder. Possible to get an A+.

People either love him or hate him... If you like teachers who are helpful and can guide you through material, DO NOT take his class. If you like teachers who are not totally clueless on the material they're teaching DO NOT take his class. You have been warned.

Not a very helpful teacher. Comes to class unprepared and has admitted to not knowing that much about 'R' (program he's teaching us to use). Showed up to our midterm 10 minutes late and half awake. If coding is not your thing and/or you'd much prefer a teacher who can actually guide you and help you understand the material, do not take his courses.

Absolutely Useless. Not only does he not come prepared to Labs/Tutorials/Sections, but he is incredibly unhelpful in providing even a shred of guidance. Condescending to his students, he is often rude and impatient, and tries to shift blame to his students even though it is he who cannot understand what he is trying to teach. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.

wow, so mean & rude when she talks to people in office hours! mayb cuz its not recorded anymore! when u ask her to clarify smth u dont understand, she says, "u tell me" ..ok, i dont understand clearly, that is y i ask u, dr.Vera! MT is too hard & tricky! VERA, PLEASE READ CAREFULY, this is the ..ONLY & FIRST STATS.. course IS & POL majors take!!

Nice prof, but having the class late on Fridays and it being stats wasn't fun.  The labs are kind of fun, you get to diddle with a computer program.  And it's a class that is geared for people who AREN'T math people (IS) so if you do the work, and ask for help from Vera you can do just fine!

Vera is a helpful teacher who is easily approachable and who wants her students to do well. She has a wicked sense of humor and shes sexy as hell! Some of the comments below seem to reveal some questionable use of the English language. I wonder if that has anything to do with their comprehension...?
She had some pretty hilarious jokes and cartoons! One of my favourite profs so far.
